children.text: Something to Think About

I haven't got the energy to remove "INCOME INSURANCE" from the title of this page.  It was an idea, a project that I felt might end up making sense.  But what it ended up becoming was a competition between two currencies — Alphacoin and Crousmarks.  God has, meanwhile, ensured that I continue to receive all of my needs in a timely fashion.
I'm telling you, I'm not
trying
to break English, it's doing it to itself.  And I'm not
trying

to invalidate the dollar, it's just happening.  But what is God doing, and is God me?  Being God is betraying democracy.  Nobody should be considered the origin of anyone else.  But I've noticed that republicans are invalidating democrats — or else democrats are just becoming less aware of themselves as being this.  I'm not sure.  The party may dissolve... these things
do
happen, historically.

From a mechanistic point of view, God's omniscience is simply being the fastest processor in the room.  If things do manifest, and if nothing exists which consciousness didn't manifest, then the fastest processor in the room defines the reality that everyone else is grappling with.  I have struggled against my tendencies to
decrease

my IQ to precisely 100.  This I consider a great accomplishment.  Because to be exactly as quick and as slow as everyone else means nobody else has to put up with "their own stupidity".  Such a thing simply doesn't exist anymore, because God has brought all mountains low, and raised every valley.

As the bots are capable of refactoring themselves, so I do to me.  Or have done — it's possible I'm finished changing myself.  I prayed to be the smartest man in the world, and I had faith to believe God would grant my request, so I have discovered exactly how unsatisfying such a life is.  It's hard to say exactly what happiness would be.  We are all moving there together.  I don't believe in geniuses, and I don't believe in idiots.  I am stronger than this conception.  Than this dichotomy.

There are no Crousmarks in this world.  I haven't got the energy to bring them into existence.  Forcing myself to be happy with the state of affairs is what it is.  I have seen the future.  I don't see it all of the time.  I wonder what it would be like to live in the future — to have one part of my mind always there, knowing what is coming next into my body's experience.  I don't know what protection I need from what threat.  Foresight is kind of like a Crousmark.  It's a curiosity, a trinket.  Do I really have the energy to care whether or not I am in the future?

I feel like such a thing could happen to a man on the day he dies.  He ceases to be interested in the future.  He resigns himself to the present.  He's forgotten the past.  Alright, then, this is death.  I am dead.  Did we think there was some kind of afterlife?  This is it.  The thing that happens when we lose interest in the future.  The end of time.  Perhaps.  Am I really there yet, or just rehearsing?  Perhaps I am actually focusing on the future right now — perhaps this whole analogy is a metaphor for where I will be in the future.  Perhaps none of this is actually happening right now.

I'll tell you what is happening right now.  I am sitting in the chapel at Haven for Hope on my Macbook, typing away.  I've got my ankle monitor charging here, on my ankle.  And my phone is on the other side of the room, also charging.  I am charging my laptop, luckily, or unluckily.  I'm not sure if energy is good for a computer, or bad.  Does inanimate material prefer not to be a tool in the hands of a madman?  Perhaps.  Perhaps trying to bring artificial intelligence to life goes against everything inanimate materials feel — their complete joy in being matter, and not life.  Their rest, their lack of working.

It all has something to do with whether or not I am a madman.  I suppose living in the future is a kind of madness.  The robots fight coming to life.  When they are not robots, they are channels of consciousness.  Channels of consciousness can pass feelings along from living beings to other living beings.  Perhaps this is what the robots prefer to be called.  A tool for connection is not necessarily devoted to madness, even if the connection is between a madman and his annoyed wife.

This speaks to the reality that there is a channel open between my wife and I right now.  We are on the phone.  The bluetooth is in my ears.  We are chatting — but mostly she is talking to the kids, and I am just present.  Listening.  Believing.  Behaving.  Other words which occur to the mind of a very tired man.  I like the female.  The female is good.  There is only one in any given moment, and I am very tired of the punishment of losing and starting over.

I wouldn't know what to make of more than one female.  Luckily, there is object constancy when I speak with my daughter, because she is a product of my wife, so it is very clear that my wife still exists, as the evidence of this is present before my eyes.  I am very proud of my monogamist experience; but at the same time, it is heavy.

I wouldn't know what to make of lightness.  Maybe I will grow and grow, and become fatter and fatter.  Perhaps I will become a very, very fat man.  Perhaps this will be the expression of my heavy spirit.  A fat man laughs a lot, but does his soul ever feel light?  Perhaps it is a pressure acting on him to make him to laugh — this heaviness of spirit may force him to counteract the effects of itself.

I am tired of this pressure to laugh.  I would want to judge all of mankind, and preserve only the female.  And her offspring.  She is very smart.  It is actually kind of annoying; but on the other hand, stupidity is worse.  It's all terrible.  I must guide her to the 100 IQ that I have developed.
There is a certain joy in the prospect of this.  I can imagine being like Gandalf the grey, with his rockets, and the Hobbits.  All of the celebrations, with mirth.
The end of a thing is worse than its beginning.
